headline: After a decade of crises between France and its former colonies, Macron chooses Kenya for the last major African trip of his presidency
description: Coming to power having promised to rebuild the relationship between France and its former colonies, Emmanuel Macron had to face a series of crises, particularly in the Sahel region. In the English-speaking part of the continent, Paris struggled to find its place.

headline: Hantavirus: After first positive case in France, government aims to 'break chain' of transmission
Pakistan emerges as a self-interested mediator in the Iran conflict
Embattled UK PM Starmer vows to prove 'doubters' wrong
description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who has been mired in the scandal around Jeffrey Epstein and criticism over his policies, sought to react to Thursday's local elections, in which his Labour party lost nearly 1,500 council seats.

headline: In Madagascar, Russia seeks a new African stronghold
description: After bursting onto the scene alongside the junta, which seized power in October 2025, by providing it with weapons and military instructors from Africa Corps, Moscow has been advancing its pawns on many fronts.

headline: In Spain, the woman priest challenging the Vatican
description: Christina Moreira advocates for women's right to the priesthood, and was excommunicated by the Vatican for seeking the role. She continues her vocation as a parish priest in Galicia, disturbing figures high up in the Catholic Church.

headline: Cannes Film Festival sees last artists walk the red carpet before the 2027 presidential election
description: The 79th edition of the film festival, opening on Tuesday, May 12, is set to be a highly political event in the city, as it is by David Lisnard, a declared presidential candidate who advocates for a right-wing primary including the far right.

The social measures tech leaders put forward to address AI's destructive potential sound familiar in Europe
description: Aware of the potentially devastating impact their technology could have on jobs and society, the leaders of OpenAI and Anthropic are advancing a range of progressive ideas aimed at preserving social cohesion.

headline: How much space should cities give to wild vegetation?
description: Bordeaux's new city council has launched a major program to weed sidewalks across the city, while in Montpellier, officials are considering expanding what it calls France's first 'wild district.'

headline: Energy transition becomes more accessible to the French middle class
description: Rising fossil fuel prices and falling used electric vehicle costs have made low-carbon solutions in housing and transport more attractive, according to a recent report by the Institute for Climate Economics.
byline: Audrey Garric and  Raphaëlle Besse Desmoulières
